orientation.scope: one joint position, center and relation orientation per accumulator perspective, shared by every slice in that perspective
orientation.perspective_independence: WHITE and BLACK compute their horizontal mirror independently from their own king and may therefore use different horizontal mirrors for the same position
orientation.formula: vertical = perspective == BLACK ? 56 : 0; horizontal = file(own_king_square XOR vertical) < 4 ? 7 : 0; oriented(square) = square XOR vertical XOR horizontal
orientation.slice_consistency: for one accumulator perspective use the same vertical and horizontal XOR for HM piece squares, CapturePair from/to squares, KBR centers and kings, and BlastRing centers and collateral squares; actor_rel and collateral_rel only relabel colors and never apply another square transform
orientation.terminal_policy: never evaluate a position after either king has exploded

bucket_selection.piece_count: all pieces present in the evaluated non-terminal Atomic position
bucket_selection.shared_selection: the same single bucket selects both the HM PSQT output and the SFNNv15 dense stack
bucket_selection.feature_input_identity: serialize this shared bucket exactly once as bucket_u8

feature_slices.name: HalfKAv2Atomic_hm
feature_slices.king_bucket_formula: for oriented own king file e-h: (7 - rank_index) * 4 + (7 - file_index)
feature_slices.training_activation_formula: for every active HM piece activate one bucket-specific training row and the matching virtual factor row; both rows contribute exactly once
feature_slices.coalesced_output_formula: coalesced[king_bucket, training_plane, oriented_piece_square, output] = bucket_weight[king_bucket, training_plane, oriented_piece_square, output] + virtual_weight[training_plane, oriented_piece_square, output], for output 0-1031
feature_slices.export_order: coalesce all 1024 accumulator outputs and all 8 PSQT outputs before applying the 12-to-11 plane mapping
feature_slices.king_plane_merge: copy training planes 0-9; initialize MERGED_KING from OPP_KING, then overwrite the oriented own-king square with OWN_KING
feature_slices.king_plane_merge_scope: apply the same per-bucket merge to accumulator outputs 0-1023 and PSQT outputs 1024-1031

feature_slices.name: AtomicCapturePair

geometry_order.edge_order: actor-relative table, then from-square and to-square in the joint oriented coordinates; OWN moves north and OPP moves south
from-square then to-square on an empty board
from-square then to-square on an empty board
from-square then to-square on an empty board

feature_slices.edge_ordinal_order: within each geometry segment enumerate oriented from-square in ascending A1=0 ordinal, then oriented to-square in ascending A1=0 ordinal
feature_slices.normal_local_index_formula: ((actor_rel * 3332 + edge_ordinal) * 6 + target_class), with actor_rel OWN=0/OPP=1 and target_class in normal_target_class_order

en_passant_edge_tables.order: for each actor_rel enumerate (oriented_from, oriented_center) lexicographically by ascending A1=0 from-square and then ascending center-square

feature_slices.pawn_edge_ordinal_formula: apply the joint per-perspective position orientation; select an 84-edge table by actor_rel; OWN contains north-moving diagonals and OPP contains south-moving diagonals; enumerate each table by ascending oriented from-square then ascending oriented to-square without another square transform
feature_slices.actor_relation_derivation: actor_rel is OWN exactly when the actual capture-actor color equals the accumulator perspective, otherwise OPP; it relabels color only and never changes the joint square orientation
feature_slices.emission_order: emit unique active rows in strictly ascending local index (therefore strictly ascending physical index); no board traversal order is observable
feature_slices.enumeration_ownership: the emitter writes only caller-owned output and scratch storage and retains no pointer, reference or span into either the Position or the output after return
feature_slices.thread_safety: pure and reentrant for concurrent reads of immutable positions: no mutable global, static or shared scratch state; callers must synchronize any concurrent Position mutation

impossible_row_policy.policy: the normal branchless rectangle stores six physical target classes for every geometric edge; EN_PASSANT exists only in the compact 28-row cold tail, so impossible edge-by-EN_PASSANT rows are absent rather than reserved, masked or serialized as zeros. This compaction does not claim every normal row is structurally reachable; the authenticated reachability bitmap remains authoritative for that separate question

semantics.target: stop at the first occupied square and emit only when that occupant is enemy
semantics.promotion: a pawn pseudocapture onto its promotion rank activates exactly one normal PAWN-actor CapturePair row; do not multiply the relation by promotion choice. A piece already promoted on the board is classified by its current KNIGHT, BISHOP, ROOK or QUEEN type as actor or target, with no history-dependent marker
semantics.en_passant: after the fail-closed metadata preconditions pass, emit one cold-tail EN_PASSANT row for every side-to-move pawn whose geometric attack reaches the EP center; do not filter pins, check evasion, self-blast or any other move-legality condition

en_passant_metadata_validation.rank: the raw EP center is on rank 6 when side to move is WHITE and rank 3 when side to move is BLACK
en_passant_metadata_validation.center: the raw EP center is empty
en_passant_metadata_validation.captured_pawn: raw_ep_center - pawn_push(side_to_move) is on board and contains an opponent pawn
en_passant_metadata_validation.actor: the candidate origin contains a side-to-move pawn, actor_rel is derived from side-to-move versus accumulator perspective, and the jointly oriented origin/center pair exists in that actor_rel's 14-edge table
en_passant_metadata_validation.last_move_history: not reconstructed from a static Position
en_passant_metadata_validation.failure_policy: if any metadata precondition fails, emit zero EN_PASSANT rows and provide zero EP candidates to downstream slices while preserving normal CapturePair enumeration; never form a partial, fallback or out-of-range EP index

semantics.relation_source: the exact unfiltered CapturePair candidates, including geometric EN_PASSANT candidates, are the sole candidate source for AtomicKingBlastEP and AtomicBlastRing
semantics.duplicates: impossible because every actor/from/to edge is unique

feature_slices.name: AtomicKingBlastEP
feature_slices.king_relation_frame: ENEMY_KING and OWN_KING are relative to the capture actor, not the accumulator perspective
feature_slices.actor_relation_derivation: actor_rel is OWN=0 exactly when the actual CapturePair actor color equals the accumulator perspective and OPP=1 otherwise; actor_color is perspective for OWN and the opposite color for OPP
feature_slices.spatial_relation_formula: for a directional class, oriented_related_king_square = oriented_center + direction_deltas_after_orientation[class_suffix], with exact file/rank adjacency and both squares on board; ENEMY_KING_N therefore means the enemy king is one rank north of the center
feature_slices.spatial_frame: directions use the joint accumulator-perspective orientation; capture-actor relation selects ENEMY_KING versus OWN_KING but never rotates or mirrors squares again
feature_slices.emission_order: emit the boolean union in strictly ascending local index (therefore strictly ascending physical index), independent of CapturePair traversal order; duplicate attackers and duplicate EP origins are idempotent
feature_slices.enumeration_ownership: the emitter writes only caller-owned fixed output and scratch storage, retains no Position or output references after return and does not expose its CapturePair traversal order
feature_slices.thread_safety: pure and reentrant for concurrent reads of immutable positions, with no mutable global, static or shared scratch state; callers synchronize any Position mutation
feature_slices.composition_seam_precondition: the internal no-reenumeration projector accepts only the exact successful AtomicCapturePair emission produced for the same snapshot and perspective; its defensive canonical-shape checks detect corruption but do not authenticate completeness of an arbitrary caller-assembled subset, and the seam is not an untrusted public input API
feature_slices.full_rectangle_policy: all 2304 center-by-actor-relation-by-class rows are serialized without compaction or holes; off-board and structurally impossible directional rows remain inactive

semantics.activation: boolean set per oriented center, actor relation and class
semantics.candidate_source: call AtomicCapturePair exactly once and project its exact validated unfiltered emission by center and actor relation, including geometric EN_PASSANT candidates; never derive candidates from attack maps, legal move generation or an independent read of EP metadata
semantics.error_propagation: if AtomicCapturePair rejects the snapshot, return its mapped error with an empty KingBlastEP emission; never retain a partial projection
semantics.multiple_attackers: never add the same relation more than once
semantics.direct_enemy_king_target: ENEMY_KING_CENTER is active only for a normal CapturePair target KING at the center; it is a pseudocapture relation rather than a legal-check signal and EN_PASSANT can never activate it
semantics.blast_threat: enemy king on one of the eight adjacent offsets
semantics.self_blast: own king on one of the eight adjacent offsets
semantics.simultaneous_king_blast: enemy-blast and self-blast relations may be active simultaneously when a center is adjacent to both kings; the candidate is self-blasting and therefore illegal
semantics.touching_kings: king adjacency and mutual king immunity are encoded by HM; kings are excluded as CapturePair actors
semantics.en_passant: EN_PASSANT_MARKER is active exactly once when at least one validated CapturePair EN_PASSANT candidate exists for the same landing center and actor relation; two origins deduplicate, the off-center captured pawn is not a center, the marker may coexist with adjacent-king classes, and malformed metadata contributes no EP marker while preserving the successful normal CapturePair projection
semantics.promotion: projection is independent of actor type: one CapturePair pawn promotion-capture candidate remains one center relation without promotion-choice multiplication, while already-promoted pieces use their current type upstream
semantics.terminal_domain: standalone enumeration requires exactly one king per color like CapturePair; king-absent explosion terminals are adjudicated before NNUE and return the mapped missing-king error with an empty emission, while preterminal king-blast threats remain represented

feature_slices.max_active_bound_proof: for each actor relation, one enemy king can occupy the center or one of eight neighbors (9 rows) and one actor-own king can occupy one of eight neighbors (8 rows), for 17; two actor relations contribute at most 34, and the single raw EP center belongs to exactly one side-to-move actor relation for a fixed perspective, contributing at most one deduplicated marker: 17 * 2 + 1 = 35
feature_slices.freeze_gate: numeric goldens with center d4 and related king d5 for direct king target, every directional offset, self-blast, touching kings, simultaneous enemy/own blast relations, both perspectives, both mirror branches and EP adjacent to either king

feature_slices.name: AtomicBlastRing
feature_slices.collateral_relation_frame: relative to the accumulator perspective, not the capture actor
feature_slices.actor_relation_derivation: actor_rel is OWN=0 exactly when the actual CapturePair actor color equals the accumulator perspective and OPP=1 otherwise; group candidates by the pair (oriented_center, actor_rel)
feature_slices.collateral_relation_derivation: collateral_rel is OWN=0 exactly when the current occupied collateral piece color equals the accumulator perspective and OPP=1 otherwise; it is never relative to the capture actor
feature_slices.spatial_relation_formula: oriented_collateral_square = oriented_center + direction_deltas_after_orientation[offset], with exact file/rank adjacency and both squares on board; offset N therefore means the collateral square is one rank north of the center
feature_slices.spatial_frame: directions use the joint accumulator-perspective orientation; actor_rel and collateral_rel only select color relations and never rotate or mirror squares again
feature_slices.emission_order: emit the boolean union in strictly ascending local index (therefore strictly ascending physical index), independent of semantic route traversal; duplicate semantic routes are idempotent, while the trusted composition seam still rejects duplicate or reordered non-canonical CapturePair input records
feature_slices.enumeration_ownership: the emitter writes only caller-owned fixed output and scratch storage, retains no Position or output references after return and does not expose its CapturePair traversal order
feature_slices.thread_safety: pure and reentrant for concurrent reads of immutable positions, with no mutable global, static or shared scratch state; callers synchronize any Position mutation
feature_slices.composition_seam_precondition: the internal no-reenumeration projector accepts only the exact successful AtomicCapturePair emission produced for the same snapshot and perspective; its defensive canonical-shape checks detect corruption but do not authenticate completeness of an arbitrary caller-assembled subset, and the seam is not an untrusted public input API
feature_slices.full_rectangle_policy: all 10240 center-by-actor-relation-by-collateral-relation-by-offset-by-class rows are serialized without compaction or holes; off-board and structurally impossible rows remain inactive

semantics.activation: boolean union of collateral outcomes for a capturable center and actor relation
semantics.candidate_source: call AtomicCapturePair exactly once and project its exact validated unfiltered emission by (oriented_center, actor_rel), including geometric EN_PASSANT candidates; never derive candidate centers from attack maps, legal move generation or an independent read of EP metadata
semantics.error_propagation: if AtomicCapturePair rejects the snapshot, return its mapped error with an empty AtomicBlastRing emission; never retain a partial projection
semantics.grouping: group the exact CapturePair records by (oriented_center, actor_rel) and count distinct oriented origins for the complete group before examining any adjacent collateral square
semantics.capturer_origin: within one (oriented_center, actor_rel) group, exclude a collateral square matching an origin only when the group has exactly one distinct origin; when the group has two or more distinct origins, retain every adjacent origin because another CapturePair outcome can capture the same center and leave that origin as collateral or a surviving pawn
semantics.collateral_snapshot: inspect the eight adjacent occupied squares in the same jointly oriented pre-capture snapshot; empty squares emit nothing, and classify occupied squares by their current piece and color rather than move history
semantics.non_pawn_classes: an adjacent current KNIGHT, BISHOP, ROOK or QUEEN activates its matching class because Atomic removes that non-pawn in the center blast, subject to the capturer-origin and en-passant exclusions
semantics.kings: an adjacent KING emits no AtomicBlastRing row because all center-to-king relations are represented only by AtomicKingBlastEP
semantics.pawns: an occupied adjacent PAWN activates ADJACENT_PAWN_SURVIVES because pawns survive collateral blast, subject to the same sole-origin exclusion and the unconditional off-center en-passant captured-pawn exclusion
semantics.en_passant_center: the CapturePair EN_PASSANT landing square is the group center; malformed EP metadata contributes no EP candidate while successful normal CapturePair groups and their ring projection remain unchanged
semantics.en_passant_captured_pawn: for every group containing a validated CapturePair EN_PASSANT candidate, always exclude oriented_center - (actor_rel == OWN ? 8 : -8), equivalently orient(raw_ep_center - pawn_push(actual_actor_color)), even when the group has two distinct origins, because that off-center pawn is removed before the blast
semantics.promotion: a CapturePair promotion capture is projected once without promotion-choice multiplication; adjacent pieces are classified by current snapshot type, so an already-promoted piece uses KNIGHT, BISHOP, ROOK or QUEEN
semantics.offboard_offsets: never active; file and rank adjacency are validated before forming a local index
semantics.terminal_domain: standalone enumeration requires exactly one king per color like CapturePair; king-absent explosion terminals are adjudicated before NNUE and return the mapped missing-king error with an empty emission

feature_slices.max_active_bound_proof: an evaluable position has at most 30 non-king collateral pieces; each can be adjacent to at most eight occupied-or-en-passant candidate centers, and each center/collateral pair fixes one actor relation, so at most 30 * 8 = 240 rows are active; king, sole-origin and off-center en-passant exclusions only reduce this total
feature_slices.freeze_gate: numeric goldens with center d4 and collateral d5 for every offset, a sole adjacent capturer, two attackers sharing a center, pawn immunity, both perspectives, both mirror branches, board edges and off-center EP removal

scope.terminal_guard: a king-absent terminal is resolved before NNUE feature enumeration

half_ka_v2_atomic_hm.proof: HM activates exactly one physical feature for every board piece; the 32-piece start position attains the bound

atomic_capture_pair.proof: each non-king piece belongs to exactly one actor relation and has at most eight first-occupied slider directions or eight knight destinations; pawn diagonals, including an empty en-passant target that substitutes one diagonal, never exceed eight total candidate edges

atomic_king_blast_ep.proof: relations are boolean per center and actor relation; only the side-to-move actor relation can contribute the one deduplicated en-passant marker

atomic_blast_ring.proof: each non-king collateral piece has at most eight adjacent occupied-or-en-passant centers and each center/collateral pair fixes one actor relation; king, sole-origin and off-center en-passant exclusions only reduce activations

aggregate.hm_virtual_rows_at_runtime: excluded because every HM virtual row is coalesced into its physical exported row
aggregate.proof: the sum is safe without claiming that the four per-slice maxima are simultaneously attainable

dimensions.full_refresh_enumerator: streaming visitor; no fixed active-list truncation

runtime_candidates: single i16 accumulator with a proven per-dimension bound
HM i16 plus relation i32 combined before clamp
numeric_policy.overflow_policy: never saturate or wrap; refresh cannot truncate active features
numeric_policy.freeze_gate: adversarial active-count and per-dimension range proof for every accepted quantized network

trainer_policy.feature_transformer: V3-specific mixed feature transformer; the generic upstream ComposedFeatureTransformer is not wire-compatible
trainer_policy.hm_outputs: 1024 accumulator columns plus 8 trainable PSQT columns
trainer_policy.relation_outputs: 1024 accumulator columns and no PSQT parameters
trainer_policy.forward: sum every slice into the 1024 accumulator outputs; derive all eight PSQT outputs from HM only
trainer_policy.serializer: write the mixed per-slice dtypes and HM-only PSQT in the declared V3 wire order

null_move_policy.reason: a null move clears the EP square without pushing the NNUE accumulator
null_move_policy.guard: store epSquareWhenComputed and force both V3 perspectives to refresh when it differs from Position::ep_square()
null_move_policy.guard_order: compare the stored and current EP squares, invalidate both V3 perspectives and only then permit any computed early return
null_move_policy.cache: relation slices are not served from the HM Finny cache; any future V3 relation cache must include EP square in its key
null_move_policy.required_fixture: EP parent -> evaluate -> null move -> evaluate -> undo null -> evaluate parent
required_fixture_assertions: the EP parent has both perspectives computed before the null move
the null move does not push the NNUE accumulator and reaches the guard with computed state
the EP mismatch invalidates and refreshes relation inputs for both perspectives before any computed early return
the HM Finny cache records a real hit for both perspectives while relation slices refresh
undo null changes the EP key back and forces a second relation refresh for both perspectives
the final parent accumulators, PSQT, transformed bytes and raw output are bit-identical to their pre-null values

wire.description: Stockfish-compatible outer header with a V3-specific mixed feature transformer
wire.sleb128_framing: COMPRESSED_LEB128 followed by a little-endian u32 compressed byte count and canonical signed values
wire.tensor_order: every weight slice is feature-major with the 1024 accumulator output dimensions contiguous inside each row; HM PSQT is feature-major with the 8 PSQT buckets contiguous inside each row

simd_permutation.load_direction: permute canonical file parameters into the active SIMD layout only after strict parsing succeeds
simd_permutation.save_direction: unpermute a copy of the active parameters before writing canonical bytes

wire.psqt_export_mapping: the 22528 by 8 PSQT tensor uses the same virtual-factor coalesce and per-bucket 12-to-11 king-plane mapping as the HM accumulator weights before serialization
wire.dense_tail: exactly the top-level dense_tail object, copied from atomic-nnue-v2

hashing.slice_hash: FNV-1a-32 over the exact descriptor bytes
hashing.feature_fold: start at zero; rotate the current u32 left by one bit; XOR the next slice hash
semantic_decision_blockers: HM virtual-factor coalesce and 12-to-11 accumulator and PSQT export goldens
AtomicCapturePair compact normal/EP split, edge ordinals, malformed-EP rejection and geometric en-passant goldens
AtomicKingBlastEP center-to-king offset and en-passant goldens
AtomicBlastRing center-to-collateral, multiple-origin, pawn-immunity and en-passant goldens
HM-only versus relation PSQT ablation decision
freeze_gates: golden indices for every class, edge, perspective, independent WHITE/BLACK mirror branch, center-to-related-square direction and HM factorized 12-to-11 accumulator and PSQT export mapping
mixed-wire canonical read, write, corruption and byte-exact round trip fixture
bit-exact C++ and Python descriptors, dimensions, offsets and hash generation
runtime loader still rejects V3 until every numeric hash is non-null and tested

production_layout_profiles.wire_identity: the complete 75084-row V3 layout and hashes; disabled slices are zero-locked and exported as canonical zero blocks

ablation_profiles.full_threats_control: separate experimental descriptor, hash, build and wire; never accepted by the release V3 loader
psqt_profiles: all-slice PSQT with a separate experimental wire identity
ablation_profiles.fairness: same dataset, train/validation split, seed, batch schedule, dense tail and compute budget

dataset_statistics.authentication: the training-run manifest is the root of trust and hashes both per-role stats sidecars, both atomic-bin-v2 manifests, both trajectory ledgers, both index-coverage files, one precommitted coverage policy and the split audit
dataset_statistics.split_policy: partition complete generated self-play trajectories by a domain-separated label-free content hash; never split positions or shards blindly
leakage_keys: V3 physical feature input plus buckets
dataset_statistics.historical_policy: existing atomic-bin-v2 datasets cannot acquire a trusted game-level split retrospectively because filtered played moves were not retained
dataset_statistics.reachable_bound_scope: at most 16 pieces per color from normal Atomic starting material
dataset_statistics.overpopulated_fen_policy: reject explicitly at the engine HM-oracle, dataset-parser and fixed-capacity trainer boundaries when either color exceeds 16 pieces or the board exceeds 32 pieces; never enumerate an overpopulated V3 sample and never truncate
